sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let's try clicking the left-panel objects
# Current state:
# - Blue checkerboard: rows 20-23, cols 18-21
# - Red block: rows 24-29, cols 18-21
# - Dark red block: rows 30-33, cols 18-21
# - Blue square: rows 34-37, cols 8-11
# - Yellow dot: rows 20-21, cols 24-25
# - Green dot: rows 38-39, cols 12-13

# Try clicking the red block (center at row 27, col 19)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 19}])
print(f"After clicking red block: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft panel objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('c', 'B', 'G', 'W', 'g') and n['pixels'] > 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if max(cols) < 30:
            print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check right panel shapes
print("\nRight panel shapes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b') and n['pixels'] > 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
